understanding.goal_understanding: Get any pushable object—the selected controllable piece or any block—to reach or exceed the target_x coordinate value from level.target_x, winning when at least one such object's x-position meets or passes it before exhausting the step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action shifts the selected piece exactly one STEP (3 pixels) in the direction; if a pushable object is adjacent in that direction, it is pushed along by one STEP. Walls block movement, with some boundaries potentially allowing or blocking pushes differently than direct movement. SELECT switches control to another object via its id. Heuristic hints like movement.{direction}_blocked indicate adjacent objects but do not guarantee outcomes—actions reveal true behavior.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_RIGHT | Selected piece at x=0 needs to reach x>=6; each MOVE_RIGHT advances x by 3, so two moves suffice (0->3->6) with all directions unblocked.
